prompt: Implement a Hospitality Operations Intelligence & Group Itinerary API for offline hotel groups and property management teams to coordinate guest movement plans while producing operational reporting from locally stored data. The backend, built with Express using Sequelize over MySQL, exposes Authentication and Account APIs that support only local username + password login (minimum 10 characters, at least 1 number and 1 symbol), role-based authorization, profile management (legal name, US mailing address, tax invoice title, preferred currency display), and self-service account deletion plus a data export entry point that packages the user's profile, activity logs, and uploaded files into a locally generated archive. Core collaboration is provided through Itinerary Group APIs that support creating groups, inviting members by pre-shared join code, and maintaining structured itinerary items such as meetup time (MM/DD/YYYY, 12-hour time), meetup location text, route checkpoints (ordered, max 30), and notes (max 2,000 chars). Groups can configure required member data fields (e.g., vehicle make/model/license plate, emergency contact name and US phone format) with per-field required/optional flags; missing required fields block check-in to a meetup. File/attachment APIs support image/document upload up to 10 MB per file with MIME-type allowlists and SHA-256 fingerprinting for deduplication; access control is enforced so only group members can read, while only owners/admins can delete. Change notifications are implemented as in-system event records (no email/SMS/push) that members can query by cursor, including what changed, who changed it, and when, with idempotency keys to prevent duplicate updates during retries. The data query and reporting center provides Reporting and Export APIs that support configurable filters and aggregations over reservations and room inventory, including occupancy rate, ADR, RevPAR, revenue mix by channel, and rollups by day/week/month, room type, and channel. Roles are Hotel Administrator (full access), Manager (property-scoped access), Analyst (read-only reporting), and Member (itinerary-only). The entire system must run on a single offline Docker host with no external dependencies, with audit logs immutable for 1 year and sensitive fields masked in logs by default.
